title: DHCP clients on VLAN 20 not getting IPs
scenario: NOC reports new PCs plugged into ASW1 ports in VLAN 20 cannot reach the network. They self-assign 169.254.x.x APIPA addresses. The DHCP server lives on R1 in VLAN 10 and is serving VLAN 10 clients just fine.

question: What is the root cause that VLAN 20 clients cannot obtain DHCP addresses?

choices.text: The DHCP pool VLAN20 has no addresses configured.
choices.explain: The pool shows 254 total addresses in the 192.168.20.0/24 range — pool is fine.

choices.text: Interface Vlan20 is missing an ip helper-address pointing to the DHCP server.
choices.explain: Vlan20 has no helper-address configured. DHCP discovers are broadcasts and must be relayed to reach a DHCP server on a different subnet. Vlan10 (where the server lives) doesn't need it the same way, but Vlan20 does.

choices.text: Interface Vlan20 is administratively down.
choices.explain: show ip interface brief shows Vlan20 status up/up.

choices.text: The DHCP server has run out of leases.
choices.explain: Pool VLAN20 shows 0 leased of 254 total — plenty of room.

rootCause: Interface Vlan20 lacks an `ip helper-address` statement. DHCP DISCOVER broadcasts from VLAN 20 hosts never reach the DHCP server because the SVI does not forward them. Vlan10 works because the clients and server share that subnet directly.

title: SSH refused to management VLAN
scenario: After a fresh switch deployment, admin Jane cannot SSH into ASW1 from her jump host. Telnet also fails. She is on the management VLAN with reachability confirmed via ping.

initialView.output: ssh: connect to host 10.0.0.5 port 22: Connection refused

showCommands.show ip ssh:
SSH Disabled - version 2.0
%Please create RSA keys to enable SSH (and of atleast 768 bits for SSH v2).
Authentication timeout: 120 secs; Authentication retries: 3
Minimum expected Diffie Hellman key size : 1024 bits
IOS Keys in SECSH format(ssh-rsa, base64 encoded): NONE
showCommands.show running-config | section line vty:
line vty 0 4
 login local
 transport input ssh
!
showCommands.show running-config | include hostname|d:
hostname ASW1
ip domain name corp.lab
username admin privilege 15 secret 5 $1$mERr$abcd1234
showCommands.ping 10.0.0.5:
Type escape sequence to abort.
Sending 5, 100-byte ICMP Echos to 10.0.0.5, timeout is 2 seconds:
!!!!!
Success rate is 100 percent (5/5), round-trip min/avg/max = 1/1/2 ms
showCommands.show ip interface brief:
Interface              IP-Address      OK? Method Status                Protocol
Vlan1                  unassigned      YES NVRAM  administratively down down
Vlan10                 10.0.0.5        YES NVRAM  up                    up
GigabitEthernet0/1     unassigned      YES unset  up                    up
GigabitEthernet0/2     unassigned      YES unset  up                    up
GigabitEthernet0/3     unassigned      YES unset  down                  down
showCommands.show tcp brief all:
TCB       Local Address           Foreign Address         (state)
*no active TCP listeners or connections*
showCommands.show ssh:
%No SSHv2 server connections running.
%No SSHv1 server connections running.

question: Why is SSH being refused?

choices.text: No domain name is configured.
choices.explain: ip domain name corp.lab IS configured per running-config — domain name is set.

choices.text: RSA crypto keys have never been generated.
choices.explain: `show ip ssh` says 'SSH Disabled' and 'Please create RSA keys'. IOS Keys: NONE. SSH cannot run without keys. Telnet also fails because vty has `transport input ssh` only.

choices.text: The vty lines block transport input.
choices.explain: transport input ssh is allowed; the issue is the SSH daemon itself isn't running.

choices.text: No local username is configured.
choices.explain: username admin privilege 15 secret is present.

rootCause: RSA keys have not been generated. `show ip ssh` reports 'SSH Disabled' and 'IOS Keys ... NONE'. Without keys the SSH server never starts listening on TCP 22.

scenario: Users on ASW1 complain of slow file transfers to the server on DSW1. The uplink Gi0/1 is up/up but throughput is poor. SW1 logs show CRC and late collisions on the uplink.

  Duplex:                half,full,auto
  Auto-negotiation:      enable

question: What is causing the throughput problem on the ASW1-DSW1 uplink?

choices.text: Cable damage causing CRC errors only.
choices.explain: CRCs on one side plus late collisions on the other is the classic duplex mismatch signature, not random cable damage.

choices.text: Duplex mismatch — DSW1 is hard-set half-duplex/100 while ASW1 auto-negotiated full-duplex/1000.
choices.explain: DSW1 gi0/24 is hardcoded `duplex half` / `speed 100`. ASW1 auto-negotiated and landed on full/1000. Late collisions on the half-duplex side and CRCs on the full-duplex side are textbook duplex mismatch.

choices.text: MTU mismatch between the two switches.
choices.explain: Both interfaces show MTU 1500; no giants are reported.

choices.text: STP is blocking the port.
choices.explain: Line protocol is up and traffic is flowing — STP is not blocking.

rootCause: Duplex mismatch. DSW1 Gi0/24 has hardcoded `duplex half speed 100` while ASW1 Gi0/1 auto-negotiated. Half-duplex side sees late collisions; full-duplex side sees CRCs and runts.
fixHint: DSW1(config-if)# no duplex half / no speed 100  (let both sides auto-negotiate)

question: Why is HSRP flapping?

choices.text: HSRP version mismatch between R1 and R2.
choices.explain: Both show v1 default virtual MAC 0000.0c07.ac32 — same version.

choices.text: R1's tracked uplink Gi0/0 is flapping, lowering R1's effective priority below R2's, then recovering and preempting back.
choices.explain: R1 tracks Gi0/0 with decrement 20. Gi0/0 line protocol is down — when it drops, R1 priority falls to 90 (below R2's 110), R2 preempts. When Gi0/0 comes back, R1 preempts again. Both routers have `preempt` set so they fight.

choices.text: Identical priority 110 on both routers causes a tie loop.
choices.explain: Equal priority resolves by highest IP and stays stable — it doesn't oscillate.

choices.text: Virtual IP 192.168.50.1 is already used by a host.
choices.explain: Nothing in the output points to an IP conflict; the state changes correlate with tracking events.

rootCause: R1 tracks Gi0/0 (decrement 20). Gi0/0 protocol is down — R1's effective priority drops to 90, R2 preempts. When Gi0/0 returns up, R1 climbs back to 110 and preempts R2. Repeat.
fixHint: Fix the underlying Gi0/0 flap (cable/transceiver/SP circuit) or remove the track if uplink instability is expected.

question: Why are R1 and R2 stuck in Speak with no Active router?

choices.text: HSRP version mismatch between v1 and v2.
choices.explain: Both sides show identical default v1 vMAC 0000.0c07.ac0a.

choices.text: Priority tie of 100 prevents election.
choices.explain: HSRP breaks priority ties by IP — it does not deadlock.

choices.text: MD5 authentication key strings differ in case (CISCO123 vs cisco123).
choices.explain: Logs show '%HSRP-4-BADAUTH'. R1 key is 'CISCO123' uppercase, R2 is 'cisco123' lowercase. HSRP MD5 keys are case-sensitive — auth fails, hellos are dropped, neither side ever sees a valid peer.

choices.text: Virtual IP 192.168.10.1 is on the wrong subnet.
choices.explain: Interfaces and virtual IP all sit in 192.168.10.0/24 (R1=.2, R2=.3, VIP=.1).

rootCause: HSRP MD5 authentication key mismatch — R1 uses 'CISCO123' and R2 uses 'cisco123'. The %HSRP-4-BADAUTH log message confirms it. Hellos are dropped, neither neighbor is discovered, both stay in Speak.
fixHint: Make both sides identical: standby 10 authentication md5 key-string CISCO123

title: NTP client stuck at stratum 16
scenario: R1 is configured to pull time from internal NTP server 10.0.0.250. NMS reports R1 as "unsynchronized" and TACACS auth on R1 is failing intermittently because timestamps drift. The server admin confirms the corporate NTP server signs with key id 2 (string CORPNTPKEY).

initialView.output:
Clock is unsynchronized, stratum 16, no reference clock
nominal freq is 250.0000 Hz, actual freq is 250.0000 Hz, precision is 2**18
reference time is 00000000.00000000 (00:00:00.000 UTC Mon Jan 1 1900)
clock offset is 0.0000 msec, root delay is 0.00 msec
root dispersion is 0.00 msec, peer dispersion is 0.00 msec
loopfilter state is 'FSET' (Drift set from file), drift is 0.000000000 s/s
system poll interval is 64, never updated.

showCommands.show ntp associations:
address         ref clock       st   when   poll reach  delay  offset    disp
 ~10.0.0.250     .INIT.          16      -     64    0  0.000   0.000  15937.
 * sys.peer, # selected, + candidate, - outlyer, x falseticker, ~ configured
showCommands.show running-config | include ntp:
ntp authenticate
ntp authentication-key 1 md5 0822455D0A16 7
ntp trusted-key 1
ntp server 10.0.0.250 key 1
showCommands.ping 10.0.0.250 source gi0/0:
Type escape sequence to abort.
Sending 5, 100-byte ICMP Echos to 10.0.0.250, timeout is 2 seconds:
Packet sent with a source address of 10.10.10.1
!!!!!
Success rate is 100 percent (5/5), round-trip min/avg/max = 1/1/2 ms
showCommands.debug ntp authentication:
NTP: authentication failed for packet from 10.0.0.250
NTP: Crypto-NAK rcvd from 10.0.0.250 — key id mismatch (rcvd key 2, configured trusted-key 1)
NTP: dropping packet, MD5 digest verification failed

question: Why won't R1 synchronize with NTP server 10.0.0.250?

choices.text: The NTP server is unreachable.
choices.explain: Ping from R1 to 10.0.0.250 is 5/5 successful.

choices.text: The NTP server is itself stratum 16.
choices.explain: Server admin confirmed server is stratum 2 with GPS reference. The scenario rules this out before the lab begins.

choices.text: NTP authentication key mismatch — R1 uses key 1, server uses key 2.
choices.explain: R1 is configured with `ntp server 10.0.0.250 key 1` and trusts key 1. Server admin confirms server signs with key 2 (CORPNTPKEY). `debug ntp authentication` shows "Crypto-NAK rcvd ... key id mismatch (rcvd key 2, configured trusted-key 1)". `show ntp associations` confirms reach=0 and refid `.INIT.` — never authenticated.

choices.text: ntp authenticate is not enabled on R1.
choices.explain: Running-config shows `ntp authenticate` is enabled.

rootCause: NTP authentication key mismatch. R1 trusts key 1, but the corporate NTP server signs with key 2. Because `ntp authenticate` is on and the server is bound to `key 1`, replies fail the MD5 check; association stays in .INIT. with reach 0.
fixHint: ntp authentication-key 2 md5 CORPNTPKEY / ntp trusted-key 2 / ntp server 10.0.0.250 key 2

title: Port stuck in err-disabled after PC connect
scenario: User reports their PC on ASW1 Gi0/5 lost link 30 seconds after they plugged in a small unmanaged desk switch to share with a phone. Port LED is amber.

initialView.output:
Port      Name               Status       Vlan       Duplex  Speed Type
Gi0/5     User-Desk-PC       err-disabled 20         auto    auto  10/100/1000BaseTX

showCommands.show interfaces gi0/5:
GigabitEthernet0/5 is down, line protocol is down (err-disabled)
  Hardware is Gigabit Ethernet, address is aabb.cc00.0105
  Description: User-Desk-PC
  MTU 1500 bytes, BW 1000000 Kbit/sec, DLY 10 usec
showCommands.show port-security interface gi0/5:
Port Security              : Enabled
Port Status                : Secure-shutdown
Violation Mode             : Shutdown
Aging Time                 : 0 mins
Aging Type                 : Absolute
SecureStatic Address Aging : Disabled

Configured MAC Addresses   : 0
Sticky MAC Addresses       : 1
Last Source Address:Vlan   : 0050.5687.aa42:20
Security Violation Count   : 1

%PM-4-ERR_DISABLE: psecure-violation error detected on Gi0/5, putting Gi0/5 in err-disable state
%PORT_SECURITY-2-PSECURE_VIOLATION: Security violation occurred, caused by MAC address 0050.5687.aa42 on port GigabitEthernet0/5.
showCommands.show running-config interface gi0/5:
interface GigabitEthernet0/5
 description User-Desk-PC
 switchport mode access
 switchport access vlan 20
 switchport port-security
 switchport port-security maximum 1
 switchport port-security mac-address sticky
 switchport port-security violation shutdown
!
showCommands.show port-security:
Secure Port  MaxSecureAddr  CurrentAddr  SecurityViolation  Security Action
                (Count)       (Count)          (Count)
---------------------------------------------------------------------------
      Gi0/5            1            1                  1         Shutdown
      Gi0/6            1            1                  0         Shutdown
---------------------------------------------------------------------------
Total Addresses in System (excluding one mac per port)     : 0
Max Addresses limit in System (excluding one mac per port) : 8192
showCommands.show port-security address:
Secure Mac Address Table
----------------------------------------------------------------------
Vlan    Mac Address       Type             Ports   Remaining Age
                                                      (mins)
----    -----------       ----             -----   -------------
  20    0050.5687.aa10    SecureSticky     Gi0/5        -
----------------------------------------------------------------------
Total Addresses in System (excluding one mac per port)     : 0
Max Addresses limit in System (excluding one mac per port) : 8192
showCommands.show interfaces status err-disabled:
Port      Name               Status               Reason               Err-disabled Vlans
Gi0/5     User-Desk-PC       err-disabled         psecure-violation
showCommands.show errdisable recovery:
ErrDisable Reason            Timer Status
-----------------            --------------
arp-inspection               Disabled
bpduguard                    Disabled
link-flap                    Disabled
psecure-violation            Disabled
security-violation           Disabled
storm-control                Disabled

Timer interval: 300 seconds

Interfaces that will be enabled at the next timeout:

Interface      Errdisable reason      Time left(sec)
showCommands.show mac address-table interface gi0/5:
Mac Address Table
-------------------------------------------

Vlan    Mac Address       Type        Ports
----    -----------       --------    -----

Total Mac Addresses for this criterion: 0

question: What put Gi0/5 into err-disabled?

choices.text: BPDU guard tripped because the unmanaged switch sent BPDUs.
choices.explain: Logs explicitly say `psecure-violation`, not BPDU guard. Also no bpduguard line in the interface config.

choices.text: Port-security violation: a second MAC appeared with max=1 and violation=shutdown.
choices.explain: Port-security max is 1 with sticky learning. When the user added a desk switch, a second MAC reached the port. Logs show PSECURE_VIOLATION and the port is in Secure-shutdown.

choices.text: Duplex mismatch caused the port to disable.
choices.explain: Duplex mismatch produces errors but does not err-disable a port.

choices.text: Storm control threshold exceeded.
choices.explain: No storm-control configuration is present and the log line names psecure-violation.

rootCause: Port-security violation. Gi0/5 had max 1 MAC with violation `shutdown`. When the user attached a desk switch, a second MAC (the phone) arrived — the switch err-disabled the port. `%PORT_SECURITY-2-PSECURE_VIOLATION` confirms it.
fixHint: Remove the extra device or raise maximum: switchport port-security maximum 2 / then shut + no shut to recover.

title: Admin's SSH session works locally but fails from corp jump host
scenario: Admin can SSH into DSW1 from a laptop on the management VLAN (10.0.0.0/24), but the corporate jump host at 172.16.5.10 always gets 'Connection closed by remote host' on the SSH banner. DSW1 reachability is fine.

initialView.output:
OpenSSH_8.9p1, OpenSSL 3.0.2
debug1: Connecting to 10.0.0.6 port 22.
debug1: Connection established.
debug1: identifying host key... ssh-rsa
debug1: ssh_exchange_identification: Connection closed by remote host
Connection closed by 10.0.0.6 port 22

showCommands.show ip ssh:
SSH Enabled - version 2.0
Authentication timeout: 120 secs; Authentication retries: 3
Minimum expected Diffie Hellman key size : 1024 bits
IOS Keys in SECSH format(ssh-rsa, base64 encoded): ssh-rsa AAAA...
showCommands.show running-config | section line vty:
line vty 0 4
 access-class MGMT-IN in
 login local
 transport input ssh
!
line vty 5 15
 access-class MGMT-IN in
 login local
 transport input ssh
!
showCommands.show access-lists MGMT-IN:
Standard IP access list MGMT-IN
    10 permit 10.0.0.0, wildcard bits 0.0.0.255 (143 matches)
    20 deny   any (27 matches)
showCommands.show users:
Line       User       Host(s)              Idle       Location
   1 vty 0     admin      idle                 00:00:00 10.0.0.55
*  2 vty 1     admin      idle                 00:00:00 10.0.0.55
showCommands.show logging:
Syslog logging: enabled (0 messages dropped, 0 flushes, 0 overruns)
    Console logging: level debugging
    Buffer logging: level debugging, 412 messages logged

Log Buffer (16384 bytes):
%SEC-6-IPACCESSLOGP: list MGMT-IN denied tcp 172.16.5.10(51234) -> 10.0.0.6(22), 1 packet
%SEC-6-IPACCESSLOGP: list MGMT-IN denied tcp 172.16.5.10(51240) -> 10.0.0.6(22), 1 packet
%SEC-6-IPACCESSLOGP: list MGMT-IN denied tcp 172.16.5.10(51247) -> 10.0.0.6(22), 1 packet
showCommands.show ip interface vlan 1:
Vlan1 is up, line protocol is up
  Internet address is 10.0.0.6/24
  Broadcast address is 255.255.255.255
  MTU is 1500 bytes
  Helper address is not set
  Outgoing access list is not set
  Inbound  access list is not set
  Proxy ARP is enabled
  Local Proxy ARP is disabled
  ICMP redirects are always sent
  ICMP unreachables are always sent
showCommands.show ssh:
Connection Version Mode Encryption  Hmac          State          Username
1         2.0     IN   aes256-ctr   hmac-sha2-256 Session started admin
1         2.0     OUT  aes256-ctr   hmac-sha2-256 Session started admin
%No SSHv1 server connections running.
showCommands.ping 172.16.5.10:
Type escape sequence to abort.
Sending 5, 100-byte ICMP Echos to 172.16.5.10, timeout is 2 seconds:
!!!!!
Success rate is 100 percent (5/5), round-trip min/avg/max = 1/2/8 ms

question: Why is SSH from the jump host (172.16.5.10) being closed?

choices.text: SSH keys are not generated on DSW1.
choices.explain: show ip ssh shows 'SSH Enabled - version 2.0' with an RSA key listed.

choices.text: vty access-class MGMT-IN only permits 10.0.0.0/24; 172.16.5.10 hits the deny any.
choices.explain: ACL MGMT-IN permits only 10.0.0.0 0.0.0.255. The jump host at 172.16.5.10 falls into the explicit `deny any` (27 matches). The SSH server accepts the TCP connection then closes it because vty filter denies the session.

choices.text: vty transport input doesn't include ssh.
choices.explain: transport input ssh is configured on all vty lines.

choices.text: No local user accounts exist on DSW1.
choices.explain: show users shows admin already logged in via 10.0.0.55 — local auth works.

rootCause: The vty access-class MGMT-IN only permits 10.0.0.0/24. The corporate jump host (172.16.5.10) is denied by the implicit deny — the connection is opened and immediately torn down at the vty filter.

title: Syslog server seeing nothing from R2
scenario: The central syslog server (10.0.0.20) is collecting logs from R1, R3, and most switches, but R2 has been silent for a week. The NOC needs R2 events for change auditing. R2 reachability to 10.0.0.20 is verified.

initialView.cmd: R2# show logging | include Trap|Logging to|Console|Buffer|Monitor
initialView.output:
Console logging: level debugging, 1421 messages logged, xml disabled,
    Monitor logging: level debugging, 0 messages logged, xml disabled,
    Buffer logging:  level debugging, 1421 messages logged, xml disabled
    Logging to 10.0.0.20  (udp port 514, audit disabled,
      authentication disabled, encryption disabled, link up),
     0 message lines logged,
     1421 message lines rate-limited,
     0 message lines dropped-by-MD,
    Trap logging: level emergencies, 0 message lines logged

logging buffered 16384 debugging

*Jun  1 11:02:14: %LINK-3-UPDOWN: Interface GigabitEthernet0/2, changed state to up
*Jun  1 11:02:15: %LINEPROTO-5-UPDOWN: Line protocol on Interface GigabitEthernet0/2, changed state to up
*Jun  1 11:14:09: %SYS-5-CONFIG_I: Configured from console by admin on vty0 (10.0.0.55)

LINEPROTO   Notifications           42
SYS         Notifications           18
SYS         Informational            6

                     filtering disabled

                     filtering disabled

                     filtering disabled

    Count and timestamp logging messages: disabled

No active filter modules.

    Trap logging: level emergencies, 0 message lines logged

              authentication disabled, encryption disabled, link up),
              0 message lines logged,
              1421 message lines rate-limited,
              0 message lines dropped-by-MD

Syslog History Table:  1 maximum table entries,
    saving level emergencies or higher
    0 messages ignored, 0 dropped, 0 recursion drops
    0 table entries flushed
  SNMP notifications not enabled

question: Why is the syslog server receiving nothing from R2?

choices.text: logging host 10.0.0.20 is missing from the config.
choices.explain: It is present in running-config.

choices.text: Reachability to the syslog server is broken.
choices.explain: Ping is 5/5 to 10.0.0.20 and the logging session shows link up.

choices.text: logging trap is set to emergencies, so only severity-0 messages are sent — the rest are ignored.
choices.explain: `Trap logging: level emergencies, 0 message lines logged`. R2 only sends syslogs of severity 0 (emergencies) to the host. Routine events (LINK, LINEPROTO, OSPF notifications) are severity 5 and never get sent.

choices.text: logging buffer is full so the host buffer is starved.
choices.explain: Buffered logging is independent of host logging.

rootCause: `logging trap emergencies` filters everything above severity 0. R2 generates plenty of events but only severity-0 messages ever leave the box. The 'Trap logging ... 0 message lines logged' counter confirms it.
fixHint: logging trap informational  (or debugging if NOC wants everything)

title: Inter-switch trunk dropping large packets, small ping works
scenario: After migrating storage to a new VLAN, backups across the DSW1-DSW2 trunk fail with timeouts. Small pings between the switches and end hosts succeed. Users say file transfers hang at random sizes. The trunk is up/up with no CRC errors.

initialView.cmd: DSW1# show interfaces gi0/24 | include MTU|input errors|giants|runts
initialView.output:
MTU 9216 bytes, BW 1000000 Kbit/sec, DLY 10 usec,
     0 runts, 0 giants, 0 throttles
     0 input errors, 0 CRC, 0 frame, 0 overrun, 0 ignored

showCommands.DSW2# show interfaces gi0/24 | include M:
MTU 1500 bytes, BW 1000000 Kbit/sec, DLY 10 usec,
     0 runts, 0 giants, 0 throttles
     14872 input errors, 0 CRC, 0 frame, 14872 overrun, 0 ignored
showCommands.DSW1# ping 10.50.50.20 size 1500 df-bit:
Type escape sequence to abort.
Sending 5, 1500-byte ICMP Echos to 10.50.50.20, timeout is 2 seconds:
Packet sent with the DF bit set
!!!!!
Success rate is 100 percent (5/5), round-trip min/avg/max = 1/1/2 ms
showCommands.DSW1# ping 10.50.50.20 size 4000 df-bit:
Type escape sequence to abort.
Sending 5, 4000-byte ICMP Echos to 10.50.50.20, timeout is 2 seconds:
Packet sent with the DF bit set
.....
Success rate is 0 percent (0/5)
showCommands.DSW1# show running-config interface gi0/:
interface GigabitEthernet0/24
 description Trunk to DSW2
 switchport mode trunk
 mtu 9216
!
showCommands.DSW2# show running-config interface gi0/:
interface GigabitEthernet0/24
 description Trunk to DSW1
 switchport mode trunk
!
showCommands.dsw2# show system mtu:
System MTU size is 1500 bytes
System Jumbo MTU size is 1500 bytes
Routing MTU size is 1500 bytes
showCommands.dsw1# show system mtu:
System MTU size is 1500 bytes
System Jumbo MTU size is 9216 bytes
Routing MTU size is 1500 bytes

question: Why do large backup frames fail while small frames succeed?

choices.text: CRC errors caused by a damaged cable.
choices.explain: Both sides report 0 CRC errors. Only DSW2 shows overruns on oversized frames.

choices.text: MTU mismatch — DSW1 trunk is set to 9216 (jumbo), DSW2 trunk left at default 1500.
choices.explain: DSW1 Gi0/24 has `mtu 9216`. DSW2 Gi0/24 is default 1500. Frames bigger than 1500 sent by DSW1 are dropped/overrun at DSW2. The DF-bit ping at 4000 bytes confirms the path can't pass that size.

choices.text: STP is blocking large frames.
choices.explain: STP forwards or blocks, it doesn't filter by size.

choices.text: Native VLAN mismatch on the trunk.
choices.explain: Output gives no indication of a CDP/STP native VLAN mismatch; the problem is purely size-related.

rootCause: MTU mismatch on the inter-switch trunk. DSW1 is configured for jumbo frames (9216) while DSW2 is at the 1500 default. Frames sized between 1501 and 9216 succeed only one direction and get dropped at DSW2 (visible as overruns).
fixHint: DSW2(config)# interface gi0/24 / mtu 9216  (and ensure all switches in the L2 storage path match)
